workflow.name: Epic Retrospective
workflow.critical: Data-driven retrospective — extract metrics and patterns from actual story files
Generate actionable learnings that enrich learnings.xml for future stories

check.action: Find epic where ALL stories are "done" but epic not yet "done" (retro pending)

check.output: Aucun epic pret pour une retro. Toutes les stories doivent etre done.

step.goal: Load all story files for this epic
step.action: Find all story files matching epic-{{epic_num}} pattern in implementation artifacts
Read COMPLETE each story file

Extract per story: - Status, number of tasks, task completion - ACs count + implementation status - Test counts (from Completion Notes) - Files modified (from File List) - Problems (from Debug Log) - Review findings (from Senior Developer Review section) - Time indicators (from Change Log dates)

Compile metrics: - Stories delivered (count) - Total tests (web + API + shared breakdown) - Total files modified - Review findings by severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW totals) - Pre-existing test failures (if any)

Analyze patterns: - What went well: successful patterns, good decisions, smooth executions - Problems encountered: each with root cause, impact, detection, fix, lesson - Recurring issues: patterns that appeared in multiple stories
Generate action items: - Specific, actionable, with owner (team/agent/user) - Distinguish: carry-forward (tech debt) vs process improvement vs convention change
Write retro file: _flow_output/implementation-artifacts/epic-{{epic_num}}-retro.md

Format: # Epic {{epic_num}} Retrospective — {{epic_title}} Date, stories delivered, test counts, commit counts
## What went well ## Problems encountered (P1, P2... with root cause + fix + lesson) ## Pre-existing test failures ## Metrics (table) ## Action items (numbered)
Read current learnings.xml

For each Problem encountered and Action item: - If it reveals a NEW pattern/antipattern not already in learnings.xml → add it - If it reinforces an existing learning → update the epic list (add this epic number) - If it contradicts an existing learning → update or remove the old one
Update last-updated attribute on flow-learnings root
Save learnings.xml
Re-read sprint-status.yaml (concurrent safety)
Update epic-{{epic_num}} status → "done"

step.critical: Keep the retro file — delete story files and epic planning file to reduce noise
step.action: Find all story files matching pattern {{epic_num}}-*-*.md in _flow_output/implementation-artifacts/
Delete each story file (e.g., 13-1-client-sidebar.md, 13-2-dashboard.md, etc.)
Do NOT delete the retro file (epic-{{epic_num}}-retro.md) — it stays
Find fix files referencing this epic in _flow_output/implementation-artifacts/ (pattern: fix-*)
Delete fix files that belong to this epic (check content for epic reference)
Find epic planning file matching epic-{{epic_num}}*.md in _flow_output/planning-artifacts/
Delete the epic planning file
Re-read sprint-status.yaml
Remove all story entries for epic-{{epic_num}} (they are done and archived in retro)
Keep the epic-{{epic_num}} entry with status "done"
step.output: **Retro Epic {{epic_num}} terminee!** Fichier: _flow_output/implementation-artifacts/epic-{{epic_num}}-retro.md Learnings: {{new_learnings_count}} nouveaux, {{updated_learnings_count}} mis a jour Cleanup: {{deleted_stories}} story files supprimes, epic planning supprime Lance /pm pour voir le prochain epic.
